title: Power BI - Security Metrics Dashboard Layout Patterns
description: Production patterns for security reporting dashboards: KPI cards with prior-period comparison, traffic light RAG status columns, multi-metric matrix layouts, sparklines as trend indicators, and drill-through from summary to detail tables

page_layout.recommended_structure:
Security dashboard page hierarchy:
1. Summary page — KPI cards in a header row, trend charts below, metric matrix in the main body
2. Monthly Detail page — full matrix of all metrics × all months, with MoM deltas, drill-through target
3. Quarterly/YoY page — quarterly comparison matrix, YoY change column
4. Individual Metric Detail page — drill-through target for a single metric showing raw data table and trend
summary_page_zones: Zone 1 (top header): KPI cards — one per key metric showing current value + vs prior month
Zone 2 (upper middle): Trend line chart — last 12 months, multiple metrics as series
Zone 3 (lower middle): Multi-metric matrix — rows=metric, columns=last 6 months, with RAG icons
Zone 4 (bottom): Period slicer (dropdown) + metric filter slicer
page_layout.visual_sizing: KPI cards: 160×100px each, arranged in a single row. Trend chart: full width, ~280px tall. Matrix: full width, ~320px tall. Slicers: 200×40px in a header strip.

basic_new_card.description: The 'New card' visual (2023+) supports showing a main measure plus a reference label and comparison value in a single compact card.
field_wells: Additional measures can be added as secondary values
basic_new_card.format_settings: Format pane > Callout value: font size 28-32pt, bold. Reference labels: smaller font. Background: white or light brand colour. Border: subtle shadow.

kpi_card_patterns.kpi_card_with_comparison_dax:
// KPI Card showing current value and vs prior month
// Two measures: one for display, one for reference
Sev5 Count Current =
    CALCULATE(
        [VulnCount],
        MetricDim[MetricName] = "Sev5"
    )

Sev5 vs PM =
VAR _Current = [Sev5 Count Current]
VAR _Prior   = CALCULATE([Sev5 Count Current], PREVIOUSMONTH('DateTable'[Date]))
VAR _Chg     = IF(ISBLANK(_Prior) || _Prior = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_Current - _Prior, ABS(_Prior)))
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_Chg), "No prior",
    IF(_Chg > 0, "▲ " & FORMAT(ABS(_Chg), "0.0%") & " vs PM",
                 "▼ " & FORMAT(ABS(_Chg), "0.0%") & " vs PM"))
// Display: card showing [Sev5 Count Current] prominently,
// with [Sev5 vs PM] as subtitle text

kpi_visual.description: The built-in KPI visual shows value, trend sparkline, and vs-target comparison. More structured than a card.
field_wells: Trend axis: DateTable[MonthYear] — draws the sparkline
Target goals: [VulnCount PM] — prior month as the target line
kpi_visual.color_coding: KPI visual auto-colors green when current > target (or < target if set to 'Low is good'). Format pane > Direction = 'Low is good' for security metrics where decreasing counts are positive.
kpi_visual.limitation: KPI visual does not support right-click drill-through. For drill-through from a KPI, use a card with a button overlay instead.

kpi_card_patterns.multi_metric_kpi_row:
Build one card per key metric in a horizontal row:
- Sev5 Count | Sev4 Count | External Vulns | Internal Vulns | Open Alerts | Avg CVSS
Each card: current value, RAG background color, vs-prior-month text label.
All cards use the same MoM % logic with different metric name filters.

traffic_light_rag_column.overview: A RAG (Red-Amber-Green) status column in a table shows a coloured indicator for each row based on current value vs threshold. Used for at-a-glance risk status.

icon_approach.description: Use conditional formatting icons (circle, diamond, flag) set to Layout = 'Data only' (icon fills the cell with no number).
icon_approach.rag_measure:
RAG Icon Value =
VAR _Val  = [Metric Value]
VAR _Chg  = [MoM Change %]
-- Higher number = worse (drives icon set: 0=green, 1=amber, 2=red)
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_Val), BLANK(),
    SWITCH(TRUE(),
        _Val >= 100 || _Chg > 0.10, 2,     -- Red: high count or >10% increase
        _Val >= 50  || _Chg > 0,    1,     -- Amber: moderate or any increase
        0                                  -- Green
    ))
setup_steps: 1. In the table visual, add [RAG Icon Value] as a column
2. Format pane > Cell elements > Icons > turn on > click fx
3. Format by: Rules, Based on field: [RAG Icon Value]
4. Add 3 rules: If value = 2 → Red circle, = 1 → Amber circle, = 0 → Green circle
5. Layout = 'Data only' (shows only icon, hides the number)
6. Optionally hide the [RAG Icon Value] numeric column from the table (set to hidden in the field well, or just don't add it as a separate visible column)

background_color_approach.description: Color the text or background of the count cell itself rather than using a separate icon column.
background_color_approach.setup:
In the table, select the count column > Format pane > Cell elements > Background color > Rules:
  If [Metric Value] >= 100 → #FF4444 (red)
  If [Metric Value] >= 50  → #FFC000 (amber)
  Otherwise                → #70AD47 (green)

traffic_light_rag_column.combined_pattern:
Production RAG column in security table:
| Metric | Current Count | MoM % | RAG | Trend |
Where RAG = icon-only conditional formatting, Trend = sparkline. This gives analysts the full picture in a scannable row.

multi_metric_matrix_layout.structure:
Rows: MetricDim[MetricName] — one row per security metric
Columns: DateTable[MonthYear] — periods as columns
Values: [Metric Value] (count), optionally [MoM Change %] as second value
Conditional formatting: background color on count column, icon on change column
multi_metric_matrix_layout.threshold_driven_rag:
// Dynamic RAG that uses per-metric thresholds from MetricDim:
RAG Status =
VAR _Val   = [Metric Value]
VAR _Red   = SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[RedThreshold], 100)
VAR _Amber = SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[AmberThreshold], 50)
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_Val), BLANK(),
    SWITCH(TRUE(),
        _Val >= _Red,   2,   -- Red
        _Val >= _Amber, 1,   -- Amber
        0                   -- Green
    ))
// SELECTEDVALUE works because the matrix row context filters MetricDim to one metric
multi_metric_matrix_layout.category_subtotals: Group metrics by category (Vulnerability / Alert / Exposure) using a hierarchy in MetricDim: MetricCategory > MetricName. Add subtotals at category level using Format pane > Row subtotals > Per row level.
multi_metric_matrix_layout.column_count_guidance: Show 6 months for a quick-view summary matrix, 13 months for a 'rolling year plus current' matrix. Add a column for 'YoY Change %' as the rightmost column to give full context without scrolling.

multi_metric_line_chart.description: Line chart with months on X-axis and multiple metrics as separate lines. Good for showing which metrics are trending up vs down.
multi_metric_line_chart.field_wells: X axis: DateTable[MonthYear], Y axis: [Metric Value], Legend: MetricDim[MetricName]
multi_metric_line_chart.issue_with_legend: When MetricDim[MetricName] is on the legend, one line per metric is shown. If metrics have very different scales (Sev4 count=800, Alert count=5), the chart is unusable. Solution: either normalise to % of max, or show only one metric per chart and use a slicer to switch.
multi_metric_line_chart.secondary_axis: For metrics with very different scales, use a Combo chart (Line + Column). Put the high-volume metric on the column Y axis and the low-volume metric on the line Y axis. The dual axis makes both readable.

trend_chart_design.normalised_trend_measure:
// Normalise each metric to 0-100 scale for a comparable multi-metric trend
Metric Normalised =
VAR _Val = [Metric Value]
VAR _Max = CALCULATE(
    MAX(SecuritySnapshots[Value]),
    ALL('DateTable'),
    ALLSELECTED(MetricDim)
)
RETURN
    IF(_Max = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_Val, _Max) * 100)
trend_chart_design.reference_line: Add target/threshold reference lines: Analytics pane > Constant line > Value = 50 (e.g. amber threshold). Label it 'Amber threshold'. This gives visual context without cluttering data labels.

dashboard_title_and_context.dynamic_title_measure:
// Dynamic title showing selected period range
Dashboard Title =
VAR _MinPeriod = MIN('DateTable'[MonthYear])
VAR _MaxPeriod = MAX('DateTable'[MonthYear])
RETURN
    "Security KPI Dashboard — " & _MinPeriod & " to " & _MaxPeriod

common_mistakes: Placing KPI cards without any comparison value — analysts cannot judge whether the current number is good or bad without context. Always add vs-prior-month or vs-threshold.
Using the same threshold for all metrics (e.g. red >= 100) — different metrics have different scales. Build per-metric thresholds into MetricDim and use SELECTEDVALUE in the RAG measure.
Trend chart with all metrics on the same Y-axis when scales differ significantly — use a field parameter slicer to show one metric at a time, or normalise.
Not adding MetricDim[SortOrder] Sort by Column — metric rows appear alphabetically instead of by risk priority.
KPI visual with 'Low is good' not set for vulnerability counts — it celebrates red (high values) when it should show red as bad.
Drill-through configured but no 'Back' button or navigation indicator on the detail page — users get lost. Always include a Back button and a breadcrumb title ('← Back to Summary').
Cards showing BLANK when the slicer has no selection or the metric has no data — add a COALESCE or IF(ISBLANK) to show 0 or a message instead of an empty card.
